SEARCH
Package com.orasaka.gateway.config

Class SecurityConfig

java.lang.Object
com.orasaka.gateway.config.SecurityConfig
@Configuration @EnableWebSecurity @EnableMethodSecurity public class SecurityConfig extends Object
Stateless Spring Security configuration for the orasaka-gateway module.

Defines the security filter chain, CORS policy, and request authorization rules. All session management is disabled (SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS); every incoming request is authenticated on-the-fly by SecurityFilter.

CORS allowed origins are environment-driven via orasaka.gateway.cors.allowed-origins in application.yml, ensuring zero hardcoded infrastructure values in compiled artifacts.

See Also:
  • SecurityFilter
  • IdentityService

  • Constructor Details

    • SecurityConfig

      public SecurityConfig(IdentityService identityService, GatewayProperties gatewayProperties, Optional<com.orasaka.gateway.config.RateLimitFilter> rateLimitFilter, GraphEngine graphEngine)
      Constructs the security configuration with its dependencies.
      Parameters:
      identityService - The service used by SecurityFilter to resolve authenticated users from the Bearer token header.
      gatewayProperties - Environment-driven gateway properties config mapping.
      rateLimitFilter - The optional rate limiting filter to register.
      graphEngine - The short-circuit graph compilation engine.

  • Method Details

    • securityFilterChain

      @Bean public org.springframework.security.web.SecurityFilterChain securityFilterChain(org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.builders.HttpSecurity http) throws Exception
      Declares the primary Spring Security filter chain.

      Security rules applied (in order of precedence):

      1. CSRF protection disabled — stateless API does not use cookies.
      2. CORS configured from corsConfigurationSource().
      3. All OPTIONS preflight requests permitted unconditionally.
      4. /api/v1/auth/login and GraphQL explorer endpoints are public.
      5. SSE streaming endpoint requires ROLE_ADMIN or ROLE_USER authority.
      6. All other requests require authentication.
      7. SecurityFilter runs before the standard form-login filter.
      Parameters:
      http - The HttpSecurity builder provided by Spring Security.
      Returns:
      The fully configured SecurityFilterChain.
      Throws:
      Exception - If any security configuration step fails during initialization.
      See Also:
      • SecurityFilter

    • corsConfigurationSource

      @Bean public org.springframework.web.cors.CorsConfigurationSource corsConfigurationSource()
      Produces the environment-driven CORS configuration source.

      Allowed origins are resolved from the GatewayProperties configuration record, enabling multi-environment deployments without recompilation.

      Returns:
      A CorsConfigurationSource applied globally to all request paths.
      See Also:
      • CorsConfiguration
